companies.name: NVIDIA Corporation
companies.note: AI GPU monopoly — ~80% training market share, Taiwan/export control exposure

companies.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC)
companies.note: Foundry for 90%+ of world advanced AI chips — only one who can make them

companies.name: ASML Holding NV
companies.note: Sole EUV machine maker — without ASML no one can make advanced AI chips

companies.name: Advanced Micro Devices
companies.note: NVIDIA challenger — MI300X GPU, 15-20% AI training market share target

companies.name: Applied Materials
companies.note: Largest chip equipment maker — China revenue 30%+, export controls key risk

companies.name: Broadcom Inc.
companies.note: Largest custom AI ASIC designer — builds Google TPU, Meta MTIA, Apple Neural Engine; also AI networking chips (Tomahawk)

companies.name: Marvell Technology Group
companies.note: Custom AI ASICs for Amazon Trainium/Inferentia, Microsoft Maia — pure-play custom silicon

companies.name: Micron Technology
companies.note: HBM3e memory — every H100/H200 needs HBM, Micron is only US HBM maker (vs SK Hynix/Samsung)

companies.name: Super Micro Computer
companies.note: AI server systems integrator — first to ship H100 servers, liquid cooling leadership

companies.name: Arm Holdings plc
companies.note: CPU architecture licensor — inside every smartphone AI chip, Apple Silicon, AWS Graviton, Qualcomm Oryon

companies.name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
companies.note: Largest US oil major — global operations

companies.name: Chevron Corporation
companies.note: Major US oil — Middle East and Kazakhstan exposure

companies.name: SLB (Schlumberger)
companies.note: Largest oil services — operates in sanctioned regions

companies.name: ConocoPhillips
companies.note: E&P focused — LNG exports, Alaska and Middle East

companies.name: Halliburton Company
companies.note: Oil services — Russia exit, Middle East heavy

companies.name: FedEx Corporation
companies.note: Air/ground freight — Red Sea beneficiary, China exposure

companies.name: United Parcel Service
companies.note: Global logistics — supply chain disruption exposure

companies.name: ZIM Integrated Shipping Services
companies.note: Container shipping — directly affected by Red Sea crisis

companies.name: Matson Inc.
companies.note: Trans-Pacific shipping — US-China trade routes

companies.name: GXO Logistics
companies.note: Contract logistics — European supply chain exposure

companies.name: Microsoft Corporation
companies.note: Azure AI — hyperscale datacenter, OpenAI partner, China exit

companies.name: Alphabet Inc. (Google)
companies.note: Google Cloud, DeepMind — submarine cable owner (Dunant)

companies.name: Amazon.com Inc.
companies.note: AWS — largest cloud, logistics, Red Sea shipping exposure

companies.name: Meta Platforms Inc.
companies.note: AI infrastructure — submarine cable owner, data sovereignty

companies.name: Equinix Inc.
companies.note: Datacenter REIT — 250 datacenters in 70 markets globally

companies.name: Lockheed Martin Corporation

companies.name: RTX Corporation (Raytheon)
companies.note: Missiles, Stinger, Patriot — Ukraine/Taiwan demand surge

companies.name: Northrop Grumman Corporation
companies.note: B-21, Space systems — classified programs exposure

companies.name: General Dynamics Corporation
companies.note: Gulfstream, submarines, Abrams — international sales

companies.name: The Boeing Company

companies.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
companies.note: Largest US bank — global operations, sanctions compliance leader

companies.name: The Goldman Sachs Group

companies.name: Citigroup Inc.

companies.name: Morgan Stanley

companies.name: Bank of America Corporation

companies.name: Newmont Corporation

companies.name: Freeport-McMoRan Inc.
